findings.where: Theorem 4.3 proof, interval check after the three-term split
findings.issue: The proof correctly chose delta < x0/2, but the sentence justifying x in (0, x0+1) only spells out the upper bound x < x0 + 1/2.
findings.evidence: It says “x0 < x0 + 1 and x < x0 + 1/2 < x0 + 1, so both x and x0 lie in (0, x0 + 1)” without explicitly mentioning x > x0/2 > 0.
findings.suggestion: Optionally add the lower-bound clause “and x > x0 - delta > x0/2 > 0.” This is advisory only; the argument is already valid.

findings.issue: The rational-sequence example uses the unproved fact that e is irrational.
findings.evidence: The caution says the rational partial sums of sum 1/n! “approach the irrational e.”
findings.suggestion: Either cite this as a known later/outside fact, or use a more elementary rational Cauchy sequence converging to sqrt(2) if that has already been established.

summary: No blocking findings. The core real-analysis arguments, exponent-law proof, seed faithfulness, and direction-brief requirements all check out.
